crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useAuth to verify UI impact
Find upstream dependency authClient to verify interface contract and type definitions
Find upstream dependency User to verify interface contract and type definitions

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The hook return property `user` was renamed to `currentUser` (line 55), but ProfileMenu.tsx destructures `{ user }` from useAuth(). After this change, `user` will be undefined, so `user.avatarUrl` and `user.name` will throw TypeError at runtime, and the null check `if (!user)` will always be true, permanently showing the LoginButton even for authenticated users.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
user: state.currentUser, // backwards compat alias
    currentUser: state.currentUser,
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed user->currentUser breaks ProfileMenu which destructures user from useAuth
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `login` function was renamed to `signIn` (line 58), but LoginForm.tsx destructures `{ login }` from useAuth() and calls `await login(email, password)`. After this change, `login` is undefined, so the form submission will throw TypeError: login is not a function, completely breaking the login flow.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
login: signIn, // backwards compat alias
    signIn,
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed login->signIn breaks LoginForm which calls login(email, password)
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `isAuthenticated` property was renamed to `isLoggedIn` (line 57), but ProtectedRoute.tsx destructures `{ isAuthenticated }` from useAuth(). Since `isAuthenticated` is now undefined (falsy), the route guard will always redirect to /login, locking out all authenticated users from every protected page.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
isAuthenticated: state.isLoggedIn, // backwards compat alias
    isLoggedIn: state.isLoggedIn,
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed isAuthenticated->isLoggedIn breaks ProtectedRoute which checks isAuthenticated
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `logout` function was renamed to `signOut` (line 59), but ProfileMenu.tsx passes `logout` to the sign-out button's onClick handler. Additionally, UserGreeting.tsx destructures `{ user, isAuthenticated }` which are both renamed. The `user` property is undefined so `user?.name` returns undefined (renders blank), and `isAuthenticated` is undefined (falsy) so the greeting never renders at all.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
logout: signOut, // backwards compat alias
    signOut,
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed logout->signOut breaks ProfileMenu sign-out button and UserGreeting display
codeSuggestions.label: bug

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact
Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact
Find components rendering Button to verify UI impact

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `onClick` prop was renamed to `onPress` (line 11), but DeleteConfirmDialog.tsx passes `onClick={onCancel}` and `onClick={onConfirm}` to Button components. Since Button now only reads `onPress`, these click handlers are silently ignored -- the Cancel and Delete buttons will render but do nothing when clicked. The dialog becomes impossible to dismiss or confirm.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
onClick?: () => void; // keep backwards compat
  onPress?: () => void;
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed onClick->onPress breaks DeleteConfirmDialog cancel and delete buttons
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `variant` prop was renamed to `appearance` (line 9) and its valid values changed from 'primary'|'secondary'|'danger'|'ghost' to 'filled'|'outlined'|'ghost'|'link'. ToolbarActions.tsx passes `variant="secondary"` and `variant="ghost"`, and CheckoutPage.tsx passes `variant="primary"`. Since Button now reads `appearance` (defaulting to 'filled'), the `variant` prop is ignored. All buttons get 'btn-filled' class instead of their intended styles. The danger variant in DeleteConfirmDialog is also lost, making the delete button look like a normal button.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
variant?: ButtonAppearance; // backwards compat alias
  appearance?: ButtonAppearance;
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed variant->appearance breaks styling in ToolbarActions, CheckoutPage, and DeleteConfirmDialog
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `size` prop values changed from 'small'|'medium'|'large' to 'xs'|'sm'|'md'|'lg'|'xl' (line 10). ToolbarActions.tsx passes `size="small"` and CheckoutPage.tsx passes `size="large"`. Since these strings no longer match the ButtonSize type, they produce CSS classes `btn-small` and `btn-large` instead of `btn-sm` and `btn-lg`, resulting in unstyled/incorrectly sized buttons. At the TypeScript level this is a type error, but at runtime the wrong class names are silently applied.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed size values from small/medium/large to xs/sm/md/lg/xl breaks ToolbarActions and CheckoutPage button sizing
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The selector `selectUserProfile` was renamed to `selectProfile` (line 88), but WorkspaceHeader.tsx imports and uses `selectUserProfile`. This will be a compile-time error (import not found), or at runtime if using JS, `selectUserProfile` will be undefined and `useSelector(undefined)` will throw. The header component will crash entirely, taking down any parent error boundary.
codeSuggestions.existingCode: export const selectProfile = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.profile;
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
export const selectProfile = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.profile;
export const selectUserProfile = selectProfile; // backwards compat
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The selector `selectUserSettings` was renamed to `selectPreferences` (line 89), and the state shape changed from `state.workspace.user.settings` to `state.workspace.preferences`. WorkspaceHeader.tsx and SettingsPanel.tsx both import `selectUserSettings` which no longer exists. Both components will fail to import the selector, crashing at the module level. The settings panel and theme indicator in the header will both break.
codeSuggestions.existingCode: export const selectPreferences = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.preferences;
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
export const selectPreferences = (state: { workspace: WorkspaceState }) => state.workspace.preferences;
export const selectUserSettings = selectPreferences; // backwards compat
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectUserSettings->selectPreferences breaks WorkspaceHeader and SettingsPanel imports
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The selector `selectItems` was renamed to `selectAllItems` (line 90) and the state changed from a flat array `state.workspace.items` to a normalized map `itemsById` with `Object.values()` conversion. ItemList.tsx and ItemDetail.tsx both import `selectItems` which no longer exists. The import will fail, and both components will crash. ItemList cannot render the list, and ItemDetail cannot find related items.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
export const selectAllItems = createSelector(
// export const selectItems = selectAllItems; // add alias for backwards compat
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed selectItems->selectAllItems breaks ItemList and ItemDetail imports
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The state field `itemCount` was removed (old line 40) in favor of a computed selector `selectItemCount` derived from `selectAllItems` (line 94). While the selector name stayed the same, the old `selectItemCount` directly read `state.workspace.itemCount` (a stored number), but the new implementation derives it from `selectAllItems`. Any component that dispatched actions expecting `itemCount` to be in the state (e.g., for optimistic updates or SSR hydration) will find the field missing. Additionally, ItemList.tsx imports `selectItemCount` alongside the now-missing `selectItems`, so it will crash on the import line before reaching the count selector.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
export const selectItemCount = createSelector(selectAllItems, (items) => items.length);
// Note: removed itemCount state field - now computed from itemsById
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Removed itemCount state field in favor of computed selector may break SSR hydration or optimistic updates
codeSuggestions.label: bug

crossFileSnippets.rationale: Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact
Find components rendering useFormValidation to verify UI impact

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The hook signature changed from positional arguments `useFormValidation(rules, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur)` to an options object `useFormValidation({ rules, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur })` (lines 41-42). RegistrationForm.tsx calls `useFormValidation(registrationRules)` passing the rules object directly as the options parameter. The hook will try to destructure `rules` from the FieldRules object, which will be undefined, causing all validation to silently fail. ContactForm.tsx and ProfileEditForm.tsx have the same issue with positional args.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
export function useFormValidation(rulesOrOptions: FieldRules | UseFormValidationOptions, validateOnChange = true, validateOnBlur = true): UseFormValidationReturn {
  const options = 'rules' in rulesOrOptions ? rulesOrOptions : { rules: rulesOrOptions, validateOnChange, validateOnBlur };
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed from positional args to options object breaks all three form components calling with positional rules argument
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The return property names were changed: `errors` -> `fieldErrors`, `valid` -> `isValid`, `dirty` -> `isDirty` (lines 30-32). All three consumer forms destructure the old names. RegistrationForm.tsx reads `{ errors, valid, dirty }` -- all will be undefined. The `!valid` check is `!undefined` which is true, so the submit button is permanently disabled. ContactForm.tsx reads `{ errors, valid }` with the same breakage -- `errors.name?.length` will throw because undefined has no property `name`.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed errors->fieldErrors, valid->isValid, dirty->isDirty breaks all three forms that destructure old names
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The utility methods were renamed: `reset` -> `resetValidation`, `touch` -> `touchField`, `isTouched` -> `isFieldTouched`, `firstError` -> `getFieldError` (lines 35-38). ContactForm.tsx calls `touch(field)`, `isTouched('name')`, and `reset()` which are all now undefined. `touch(field)` will throw TypeError. RegistrationForm.tsx and ProfileEditForm.tsx call `firstError('email')` and `reset()` which are also undefined, crashing on invocation. Every form component that uses these methods will throw at runtime.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed reset/touch/isTouched/firstError to resetValidation/touchField/isFieldTouched/getFieldError breaks all form consumers
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `colors.primary` token was renamed to `colors.brand` (line 6), along with `primaryHover` -> `brandHover` and `primaryLight` -> `brandLight` (lines 7-8). PrimaryButton.tsx uses `theme.colors.primary` for its background color, which is now undefined. The button will render with no background color (transparent), making it invisible or unreadable against the page background. The hover state is also broken since `primaryHover` no longer exists.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed colors.primary->brand breaks PrimaryButton background color rendering
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The spacing tokens were renamed from short names (`xs`, `sm`, `md`, `lg`, `xl`) to full names (`xsmall`, `small`, `medium`, `large`, `xlarge`) on lines 19-23. PrimaryButton.tsx uses `theme.spacing.sm` and `theme.spacing.md` for padding. Card.tsx uses `theme.spacing.lg`. DropdownMenu.tsx uses `theme.spacing.xs` and `theme.spacing.sm`. FormField.tsx uses `theme.spacing.md` and `theme.spacing.xs`. All these references return undefined, resulting in `padding: undefined` which collapses all spacing to 0, breaking the layout of every component using spacing tokens.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed spacing tokens xs/sm/md/lg/xl to xsmall/small/medium/large/xlarge breaks 5+ components
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `breakpoints` type changed from a named object `{ mobile, tablet, desktop, wide }` to a positional array `[string, string, string, string]` (line 25). ResponsiveGrid.tsx accesses `theme.breakpoints.tablet` and `theme.breakpoints.desktop` for media queries. Since breakpoints is now an array, `.tablet` and `.desktop` return undefined, producing `@media (min-width: undefined)` which is an invalid media query. The grid will never switch to multi-column layout on wider screens.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode: breakpoints: { mobile: string; tablet: string; desktop: string; wide: string };
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Changed breakpoints from named object to array breaks ResponsiveGrid media queries
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The `fonts.body` token was renamed to `fonts.sans` (line 27). PrimaryButton.tsx, DropdownMenu.tsx, and FormField.tsx all use `theme.fonts.body` for fontFamily. Since `fonts.body` is now undefined, `fontFamily: undefined` is set, which causes the browser to fall back to its default serif font (typically Times New Roman). This creates a visually jarring inconsistency where some components show the intended sans-serif font and others show serif.
codeSuggestions.improvedCode:
body: string; // backwards compat alias
    sans: string;
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed fonts.body->fonts.sans breaks font rendering in PrimaryButton, DropdownMenu, and FormField
codeSuggestions.label: bug

codeSuggestions.suggestionContent: The shadow tokens were renamed: `shadows.card` -> `shadows.elevation1`, `shadows.dropdown` -> `shadows.elevation2`, `shadows.sm` -> `shadows.subtle` (lines 32-35). Card.tsx uses `theme.shadows.card` for its box shadow, which is now undefined -- cards render flat with no shadow. DropdownMenu.tsx uses `theme.shadows.dropdown` which is also undefined, so dropdown menus appear without any shadow or visual separation from the background, making them hard to distinguish from the page content.
codeSuggestions.oneSentenceSummary: Renamed shadows.card/dropdown/sm to elevation1/elevation2/subtle breaks Card and DropdownMenu box shadows
codeSuggestions.label: bug
